attacking_style: Explosive, ultra-late sprint attacker who launches in the final 50 meters of steep finishes, targeting stage placings and bonus seconds rather than breakaway opportunities.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with limited dedicated lead-out support, relying on individual positioning in elite group finishes rather than orchestrated team setup.
terrain_preference: Steep uphill finishes where punchy acceleration can create separation; struggles when stronger sprinters or GC riders can remain positioned on his wheel.
vulnerability: Susceptible to being neutralized by wheel-sucking tactics from better-positioned or stronger finishers; late attacks can be countered by riders with superior kick.
key_patterns: Launches attacks in final 50 meters on steep gradients (2 observations)
Targets bonus seconds and stage placings on uphill sprint finishes (2 observations)
GC contenders and sprinters successfully counter by holding his wheel (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: both observations from Vuelta finishes show similar tactical approach but insufficient recent data to assess performance trend
